title: Sō Percussion

infobox.value: [Cantaloupe Music](/wiki/Cantaloupe_Music "Cantaloupe Music") [Nonesuch Records](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records") [Thrill Jockey](/wiki/Thrill_Jockey "Thrill Jockey") [Brassland Records](/wiki/Brassland_Records "Brassland Records") [New Amsterdam Records](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_Records "New Amsterdam Records")
Josh Quillen Adam Sliwinski Jason Treuting Eric Cha-Beach
Tim Feeney Todd Meehan Douglas Perkins [Lawson White](/wiki/Lawson_White "Lawson White")
is a Grammy winning American percussion quartet formed in 1999 and based in New York City.

Since 2014, the members of Sō Percussion have served as the [Edward T. Cone](/wiki/Edward_T._Cone "Edward T. Cone") performers-in-residence at Princeton University.[[41]](#cite_note-41) The group has commissioned and recorded major works from Princeton composition faculty [Steven Mackey](/wiki/Steven_Mackey "Steven Mackey"), [Paul Lansky](/wiki/Paul_Lansky "Paul Lansky"), and [Dan Trueman](/wiki/Dan_Trueman "Dan Trueman"), as well as collaborating many times with the Princeton Sound Kitchen and Princeton Laptop Orchestra (PLOrk).[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)

The annual Sō Percussion Summer Institute (SōSI), founded in 2009, also takes place on Princeton's campus. The program features teaching and performing with the members of Sō Percussion and with Princeton faculty and student composers. Each year includes a number of concerts on Princeton's campus, in New York, outdoors in downtown Princeton, and in Small World coffee shop.[[44]](#cite_note-44)[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[48]](#cite_note-48)

In 2011 the members of Sō Percussion established the first percussion department at the [Bard College Conservatory of Music](/wiki/Bard_College_Conservatory_of_Music "Bard College Conservatory of Music"). The undergraduate program is a five-year double degree. Sō Percussion members Eric Cha-Beach and Jason Treuting are current co-directors of the department, together with timpanist Jason Haaheim[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)[[52]](#cite_note-52)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Sō Percussion](/w/index.php?title=S%C5%8D_Percussion&redirect=no "Sō Percussion"))

Percussion ensemble based in NYC

| **Sō Percussion** | |
| --- | --- |
| [Members of Sō Percussion play at the Miller Theatre in New York in February, 2020](/wiki/File:Caroline_Shaw_with_Attacca_Quartet_and_So_Percussion_at_Miller_Theater_-_49517620871.jpg "Members of Sō Percussion play at the Miller Theatre in New York in February, 2020") Members of Sō Percussion play at the [Miller Theatre](/wiki/Miller_Theatre "Miller Theatre") in New York in February, 2020 | |
| Background information | |
| Origin | [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York") |
| Genres | Percussion |
| Labels | [Cantaloupe Music](/wiki/Cantaloupe_Music "Cantaloupe Music") [Nonesuch Records](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records") [Thrill Jockey](/wiki/Thrill_Jockey "Thrill Jockey") [Brassland Records](/wiki/Brassland_Records "Brassland Records") [New Amsterdam Records](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_Records "New Amsterdam Records") |
|  | |
| Members | Josh Quillen Adam Sliwinski Jason Treuting Eric Cha-Beach |
|  | |
| Past members | Tim Feeney Todd Meehan Douglas Perkins [Lawson White](/wiki/Lawson_White "Lawson White") |
|  | |
| Website | [Official Site](http://www.sopercussion.com) |

**Sō Percussion** is a Grammy winning American percussion quartet formed in 1999 and based in New York City.

Composed of Josh Quillen, Adam Sliwinski, Jason Treuting, and Eric Cha-Beach, the group is well known for recording and touring internationally and for their work with composers such as [Steve Reich](/wiki/Steve_Reich "Steve Reich"), [David Lang](/wiki/David_Lang_(composer) "David Lang (composer)"), [Caroline Shaw](/wiki/Caroline_Shaw "Caroline Shaw"), [Bryce Dessner](/wiki/Bryce_Dessner "Bryce Dessner"), [Julia Wolfe](/wiki/Julia_Wolfe "Julia Wolfe"), [Vijay Iyer](/wiki/Vijay_Iyer "Vijay Iyer"), [Fred Frith](/wiki/Fred_Frith "Fred Frith"), [Angélica Negrón](/wiki/Ang%C3%A9lica_Negr%C3%B3n "Angélica Negrón"), [Nathalie Joachim](/wiki/Nathalie_Joachim "Nathalie Joachim"), [Dan Trueman](/wiki/Dan_Trueman "Dan Trueman"), [Tristan Perich](/wiki/Tristan_Perich "Tristan Perich"), [Paul Lansky](/wiki/Paul_Lansky "Paul Lansky"), [Claire Rousay](/wiki/Claire_Rousay "Claire Rousay"), Shodekeh Talifero, [Leilehua Lanzilotti](/wiki/Leilehua_Lanzilotti "Leilehua Lanzilotti"), [Steven Mackey](/wiki/Steven_Mackey "Steven Mackey"), Kendall K. Williams, [Shara Nova](/wiki/Shara_Nova "Shara Nova"), [Martin Bresnick](/wiki/Martin_Bresnick "Martin Bresnick"), [Oscar Bettison](/wiki/Oscar_Bettison "Oscar Bettison"), Olivier Tarpaga, [Evan Ziporyn](/wiki/Evan_Ziporyn "Evan Ziporyn"), and [Arvo Pärt](/wiki/Arvo_P%C3%A4rt "Arvo Pärt"). Originally formed when the members were students of [Robert van Sice](/wiki/Robert_van_Sice "Robert van Sice") at the [Yale School of Music](/wiki/Yale_School_of_Music "Yale School of Music"), the group also continues to play works from the standard repertoire of percussion ensemble music—including works by composers such as [John Cage](/wiki/John_Cage "John Cage"), [Julius Eastman](/wiki/Julius_Eastman "Julius Eastman"), [Pauline Oliveros](/wiki/Pauline_Oliveros "Pauline Oliveros"), [George Crumb](/wiki/George_Crumb "George Crumb"), and [Iannis Xenakis](/wiki/Iannis_Xenakis "Iannis Xenakis"). In addition to their work with composers, the members of Sō Percussion produce original music, including large scale evening-length works.

The group is also known for their use of non-standard instruments and found sounds in performance and on recordings, such as scrap metal, rocks, flower pots, and an [amplified cactus](/wiki/Amplified_cactus "Amplified cactus").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Sō Percussion has released albums on [Cantaloupe Music](/wiki/Cantaloupe_Music "Cantaloupe Music"), [Nonesuch Records](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records"), [Thrill Jockey](/wiki/Thrill_Jockey "Thrill Jockey"), [Brassland Records](/wiki/Brassland_Records "Brassland Records"), and [New Amsterdam Records](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_Records "New Amsterdam Records"). The group endorses [Zildjian](/wiki/Zildjian "Zildjian"), [Vic Firth](/wiki/Vic_Firth "Vic Firth"), [Remo](/wiki/Remo "Remo") and [Pearl](/wiki/Pearl_Drums "Pearl Drums")/[Adams](/wiki/Adams_Musical_Instruments "Adams Musical Instruments").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Naming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Naming")]

The group's name was suggested by Jenise Treuting, Jason Treuting's sister.

Jenise writes,

> The Sō in Sō Percussion comes from [奏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%A5%8F "wikt:奏"), the second character in the compound Japanese word [演奏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%BC%94%E5%A5%8F "wikt:演奏") (ensou), to perform music. By itself, so means "to play an instrument." But it can also mean "to be successful," "to determine a direction and move forward," and "to present to the gods or ruler." Scholars have suggested that the latter comes from the character's etymology, which included the element "to offer with both hands." 奏 is a bold, straightforward character, but lends itself to calligraphy with a certain energy that gives *so* a springy, delicate look.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Collaborations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Collaborations")]

Sō Percussion frequently collaborates with other musicians and performers from around the world, including [The National](/wiki/The_National_(band) "The National (band)") (appearing on Grammy-winning album '[Sleep Well Beast](/wiki/Sleep_Well_Beast "Sleep Well Beast")'), [Buke and Gase](/wiki/Buke_and_Gase "Buke and Gase"), [Shara Nova](/wiki/Shara_Nova "Shara Nova"), [Dave Douglas](/wiki/Dave_Douglas_(trumpeter) "Dave Douglas (trumpeter)"), [Matmos](/wiki/Matmos "Matmos"), [Medeski Martin & Wood](/wiki/Medeski_Martin_%26_Wood "Medeski Martin & Wood"), [Dan Deacon](/wiki/Dan_Deacon "Dan Deacon"), [The Dirty Projectors](/wiki/The_Dirty_Projectors "The Dirty Projectors"), [Glenn Kotche](/wiki/Glenn_Kotche "Glenn Kotche"), [Bobby Previte](/wiki/Bobby_Previte "Bobby Previte"), [Kid Millions/Man Forever](http://www.thrilljockey.com/thrill/Man-Forever#.VcJpRGRViko), [Eli Keszler](/wiki/Eli_Keszler "Eli Keszler"), [Emily Johnson](/wiki/Emily_Johnson "Emily Johnson"), [Ain Gordon](/wiki/Ain_Gordon "Ain Gordon"), [Shen Wei](/wiki/Shen_Wei "Shen Wei"), and [The Princeton Laptop Orchestra](/wiki/PLOrk "PLOrk"). Recent collaborative projects include the creation with [Jad Abumrad](/wiki/Jad_Abumrad "Jad Abumrad") of a score to the [Radiolab](/wiki/Radiolab "Radiolab") story "[The Heartbeat](http://www.radiolab.org/story/heartbeat/)," part of the episode "Radiolab Live: Telltale Hearts featuring Oliver Sacks," produced by Molly Webster and performed live by Sō Percussion at Brooklyn Academy of Music's "RadioLoveFest.".[[8]](#cite_note-8) So Percussion worked with composer [West Dylan Thordson](/wiki/West_Dylan_Thordson "West Dylan Thordson") as contributing performers on the score of *[The Jinx: The Life and Deaths of Robert Durst](/wiki/The_Jinx:_The_Life_and_Deaths_of_Robert_Durst "The Jinx: The Life and Deaths of Robert Durst")*, a 2015 [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") documentary miniseries about [Robert Durst](/wiki/Robert_Durst "Robert Durst").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Sō Percussion* (2004)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* Steve Reich: *Drumming* (2005)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* *Amid the Noise* (2006) \*CD/DVD[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* *Five (and-a-half) Gardens* — with Trollstilt (2007)
* *Treasure State* — with Matmos (2010)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* Paul Lansky: *Threads* (2011)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* Steve Reich: *WTC 9/11* - Mallet Quartet Recording (2011) \*CD/DVD[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* Steve Mackey: *It Is Time* (2011) \*CD/DVD[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* Martin Bresnick: *Caprichos Enfaticos* with Lisa Moore, piano (2011)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* *Bad Mango* with Dave Douglas, trumpet (2011)[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* David Lang: *The Woodmans - Music From the Film* (2011)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* *Amid the Noise Remixes* (2011)[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* Cage 100: Bootleg Series (2012)[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* *Where (we) Live* (2012)[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* *neither Anvil nor Pulley* (2013)[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* Cenk Ergun: *Nana* - Proximity Recording (2014)[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* Man Forever: *Ryonen* (2014)[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* Bobby Previte: *TERMINALS* (2014)[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* Bryce Dessner: *Music for Wood and Strings* (2015)[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* Glenn Kotche: *Drumkit Quartets* (2016)[[28]](#cite_note-28)
* Steve Reich and So Percussion: *Drumming Live* (2017)[[29]](#cite_note-29)
* Color Theory, with PRISM Sax Quartet (2017)[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* Dan Trueman: *Songs That Are Hard to Sing* - with JACK Quartet (2019)[[31]](#cite_note-31)
* *A Record Of...* - with Buke and Gase (2021)[[32]](#cite_note-32)
* Caroline Shaw: *Narrow Sea* - with Dawn Upshaw and Gil Kalish (2021)[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* Julius Eastman: *Stay On It* - with MEDIAQUEER, [Adam Tendler](/wiki/Adam_Tendler "Adam Tendler"), Beth Meyers, Grey Mcmurray, Shelley Washington & Alex Sopp (2021)[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* Paul Lansky: *Angles* (2021)[[35]](#cite_note-35)
* *Steve Reich* with NEXUS Percussion (2021)[[36]](#cite_note-36)
* *Let The Soil Play Its Simple Part* - with Caroline Shaw (2021)[[37]](#cite_note-37)
* Julia Wolfe: *Forbidden Love* (2022)[[38]](#cite_note-38)
* *Individuate* - With Darian Thomas, Bergamot Quartet, Shelby Blezinger-McCay, and Kasey Blezinger (2022)[[39]](#cite_note-39)
* *[Rectangles and Circumstance](/wiki/Rectangles_and_Circumstance "Rectangles and Circumstance")* - with Caroline Shaw (2024)[[40]](#cite_note-40)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education")]

**Princeton University**  
Since 2014, the members of Sō Percussion have served as the [Edward T. Cone](/wiki/Edward_T._Cone "Edward T. Cone") performers-in-residence at Princeton University.[[41]](#cite_note-41) The group has commissioned and recorded major works from Princeton composition faculty [Steven Mackey](/wiki/Steven_Mackey "Steven Mackey"), [Paul Lansky](/wiki/Paul_Lansky "Paul Lansky"), and [Dan Trueman](/wiki/Dan_Trueman "Dan Trueman"), as well as collaborating many times with the Princeton Sound Kitchen and Princeton Laptop Orchestra (PLOrk).[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)

**Sō Percussion Summer Institute**  
The annual Sō Percussion Summer Institute (SōSI), founded in 2009, also takes place on Princeton's campus. The program features teaching and performing with the members of Sō Percussion and with Princeton faculty and student composers. Each year includes a number of concerts on Princeton's campus, in New York, outdoors in downtown Princeton, and in Small World coffee shop.[[44]](#cite_note-44)[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[48]](#cite_note-48)

**Bard College Conservatory**  
In 2011 the members of Sō Percussion established the first percussion department at the [Bard College Conservatory of Music](/wiki/Bard_College_Conservatory_of_Music "Bard College Conservatory of Music"). The undergraduate program is a five-year double degree. Sō Percussion members Eric Cha-Beach and Jason Treuting are current co-directors of the department, together with timpanist Jason Haaheim[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)[[52]](#cite_note-52)

## Original music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Original music")]

**Works with Caroline Shaw** (2021-2024)  
The members of Sō Percussion co-composed two sets of songs together with singer and composer [Caroline Shaw](/wiki/Caroline_Shaw "Caroline Shaw"), each released as an album on [Nonesuch Records](/wiki/Nonesuch_Records "Nonesuch Records") - *Let the Soil Play Its Simple Part* (2021) and *[Rectangles and Circumstance](/wiki/Rectangles_and_Circumstance "Rectangles and Circumstance")* (2024), the latter of which received a 2025 [Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Chamber_Music/Small_Ensemble_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance").[[53]](#cite_note-53)[[54]](#cite_note-54)

**From Out a Darker Sea** (2017)  
From Out a Darker Sea explores the former coal mining communities of East Durham, UK in a collaboration with Forma Arts and [Amber Films](/wiki/Amber_Films "Amber Films").[[55]](#cite_note-55)

**A Gun Show** (2016)  
A Gun Show is an exploration of American gun culture using music, video, spoken text, and movement. The project was created collaboratively by the members of Sō Percussion together with director [Ain Gordon](/wiki/Ain_Gordon "Ain Gordon") and choreographer [Emily Johnson](/wiki/Emily_Johnson "Emily Johnson").[[56]](#cite_note-56)[[57]](#cite_note-57)

**Where (we) Live** (2013)  
Where (we) Live was an original project exploring ideas about home and community, created together with guitarist Grey mcmurray, director [Ain Gordon](/wiki/Ain_Gordon "Ain Gordon"), choreographer [Emily Johnson](/wiki/Emily_Johnson "Emily Johnson"), and video designer [Martin Schmidt](/wiki/Matmos "Matmos").[[58]](#cite_note-58)

**2wice - Fifth Wall** (2012)  
Members of Sō Percussion composed the score for 'Fifth Wall' - an [iPad](/wiki/IPad "IPad")-based performance by dancer [Jonah Bokaer](/wiki/Jonah_Bokaer "Jonah Bokaer"), published by dance magazine [2wice](/wiki/2wice "2wice").[[59]](#cite_note-59)[[60]](#cite_note-60)

**Shen Wei - Undivided/Divided** (2011)  
The members of Sō Percussion composed the music for Undivided/Divided with choreographer [Shen Wei](/wiki/Shen_Wei "Shen Wei"), which premiered at the [Park Avenue Armory](/wiki/Seventh_Regiment_Armory "Seventh Regiment Armory") in November, 2011.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

**Martin Kersels: 5 Songs** (2010)  
In 2010 the [Whitney Museum](/wiki/Whitney_Museum "Whitney Museum") commissioned the members of Sō Percussion to write new original music for performance in connection with [Martin Kersels](/wiki/Martin_Kersels "Martin Kersels")' sculpture project for the Whitney Biennial: 5 Songs.[[62]](#cite_note-62)

**Imaginary City** (2009)  
Inspired by the [Italo Calvino](/wiki/Italo_Calvino "Italo Calvino") novel [Invisible Cities](/wiki/Invisible_Cities "Invisible Cities"), Imaginary City used as inspiration the six cities that are home to the presenters that commissioned it: The [Brooklyn Academy of Music](/wiki/Brooklyn_Academy_of_Music "Brooklyn Academy of Music") in Brooklyn, NY; The Myrna Loy Center in Helena, MT; [The Cleveland Museum of Art](/wiki/The_Cleveland_Museum_of_Art "The Cleveland Museum of Art") in Cleveland, OH; The Flynn Center in Burlington, VT; Diverseworks Art Space in Houston, TX; and The Newman Center in Denver, CO. The project again included original video created by Jenise Treuting as well as theatrical direction by Rinde Eckert.
[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)

**Music for Trains** (2008)  
In 2008 Sō Percussion developed the Music for Trains project in southern [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont"). The month-long residency project centered around performances in and around the towns of [Brattleboro](/wiki/Brattleboro "Brattleboro") and [Bellows Falls](/wiki/Bellows_Falls "Bellows Falls"), including concerts in the train stations of those two towns, pre-recorded mp3 players listened to on the trains, and materials gathered from the local community. The project also included original video created by Jenise Treuting and an on-stage sculpture created by local artist Ahren Ahrenholz.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66)

**Amid the Noise** (2006)  
A series of short pieces written by group member Jason Treuting, Amid the Noise was the first project of original music created and recorded by members of Sō Percussion. The project also features videos created by video artist Jenise Treuting, which were included in the CD/DVD release of 2006.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Awards")]

* *[Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Chamber_Music/Small_Ensemble_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance")* - 2025
* *Bessie Award for Outstanding Musical Composition/Sound Design* - 2016[[68]](#cite_note-68)
* *American Music Center Trailblazer Award* - 2011[[69]](#cite_note-69)
* *Chamber Music America/ASCAP Award for Adventurous Programming* - 2004, 2006, 2010[[70]](#cite_note-70)[[71]](#cite_note-71)
* *ASCAP John Cage Award* - 2009[[72]](#cite_note-72)
* *International Percussion Competition Luxembourg* 2nd Place - 2005[[73]](#cite_note-73)

## Selected works commissioned

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=So_Percussion&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Selected works commissioned")]

* Dennis DeSantis: *Shifty* (2000)[[74]](#cite_note-74)
* [David Lang](/wiki/David_Lang_(composer) "David Lang (composer)"): *the so-called laws of nature* (2002)[[75]](#cite_note-75)
* [Paul Lansky](/wiki/Paul_Lansky "Paul Lansky"): *Threads* (2006)[[76]](#cite_note-76)
* [Martin Bresnick](/wiki/Martin_Bresnick "Martin Bresnick"): *Caprichos Enfaticos* (2007)[[77]](#cite_note-77)
* [Steven Mackey](/wiki/Steven_Mackey "Steven Mackey"): *It Is Time* (2010)[[78]](#cite_note-78)
* [Dan Trueman](/wiki/Dan_Trueman "Dan Trueman"): *neither anvil nor pulley* (2010)[[79]](#cite_note-79)
* [Steve Reich](/wiki/Steve_Reich "Steve Reich"): *Mallet Quartet* (2010)[[80]](#cite_note-80)
* [Bobby Previte](/wiki/Bobby_Previte "Bobby Previte"): *Terminals* (2011)[[81]](#cite_note-81)
* [Dan Deacon](/wiki/Dan_Deacon "Dan Deacon"): *Ghostbuster Cook: The Origin of the Riddler* (2011)[[82]](#cite_note-82)
* [Glenn Kotche](/wiki/Glenn_Kotche "Glenn Kotche"): *Drumkit Quartets* (2011)[[83]](#cite_note-83)
* [David Lang](/wiki/David_Lang_(composer) "David Lang (composer)"): *man made* (2013)[[84]](#cite_note-84)
* [Bryce Dessner](/wiki/Bryce_Dessner "Bryce Dessner"): *Music for Wood and Strings* (2013)[[85]](#cite_note-85)
* [Shara Nova](/wiki/Shara_Nova "Shara Nova")/So Percussion: *Timeline* (2015)[[86]](#cite_note-86)
* [Paul Lansky](/wiki/Paul_Lansky "Paul Lansky"): *Springs* (2016)[[87]](#cite_note-87)
* [Caroline Shaw](/wiki/Caroline_Shaw "Caroline Shaw"): *Narrow Sea* (2017) with Dawn Upshaw[[88]](#cite_note-88)
* [Donnacha Dennehy](/wiki/Donnacha_Dennehy "Donnacha Dennehy"): *Broken Unison* (2017)[[89]](#cite_note-89)
* [Dan Trueman](/wiki/Dan_Trueman "Dan Trueman"): *Songs That Are Hard to Sing* (2017) with JACK Quartet[[90]](#cite_note-90)
* [Vijay Iyer](/wiki/Vijay_Iyer "Vijay Iyer"): *TORQUE* (2018)[[91]](#cite_note-91)
* [Julia Wolfe](/wiki/Julia_Wolfe "Julia Wolfe"): *Forbidden Love* (2019)[[92]](#cite_note-92)
* Shodekeh Talifero: *Vodalities, Paradigms of Consciousness for the Human Voice* (2021)[[93]](#cite_note-93)
* [Nathalie Joachim](/wiki/Nathalie_Joachim "Nathalie Joachim"): *Note to Self* (2021)[[94]](#cite_note-94)
* [Bora Yoon](/wiki/Bora_Yoon_(American_musician) "Bora Yoon (American musician)"): *the wonder that's keeping the stars apart* (2022)[[95]](#cite_note-95)
* Olivier Tarpaga: *Fēfē* (2023)[[96]](#cite_note-96)
* [Angélica Negrón](/wiki/Ang%C3%A9lica_Negr%C3%B3n "Angélica Negrón"): *Inward Pieces* (2023)[[97]](#cite_note-97)
* [Vijay Iyer](/wiki/Vijay_Iyer "Vijay Iyer"): *Dharma Eye* (2023)[[98]](#cite_note-98)
* [Leilehua Lanzilotti](/wiki/Leilehua_Lanzilotti "Leilehua Lanzilotti"): *Sending Messages* (2024)[[99]](#cite_note-99)
* [Claire Rousay](/wiki/Claire_Rousay "Claire Rousay"): *In Places* (2024)[[100]](#cite_note-100)
